title: Lithocarpus clementianus
taxonomy: Lithocarpus clementianus is classified as a species of tree in the family Fagaceae. This family includes other types of trees such as oak and beech. Lithocarpus clementianus is a member of the genus Lithocarpus, which contains various species of evergreen trees. The genus Lithocarpus is closely related to the genus Quercus, which includes oak trees. The classification of Lithocarpus clementianus is a part of the broader classification of angiosperms within the plant kingdom.

properties: Lithocarpus clementianus, also known as the Clement's nut tree, has been found to possess various therapeutic properties, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. intro: Lithocarpus clementianus, also known as Clements' lily pine or Clements' lily tree, is a species of evergreen tree in the beech family Fagaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and promote overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Lithocarpus clementianus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in various extracts, such as capsules and infusions, to support respiratory health and immune function.
